facts.title: 1. SecureEnclaveIdentity (Core Identity Management)

facts.statement: 1. **Private Key Isolation**: P-256 private keys are generated and stored exclusively in the Secure Enclave hardware - they cannot be extracted or cloned
facts.title: Hardware Security Guarantees

facts.statement: 2. **Tamper Resistance**: Secure Enclave provides hardware-level protection against physical and software attacks
facts.title: Hardware Security Guarantees

facts.statement: 3. **Device Binding**: Keys are bound to the specific device's Secure Enclave and cannot be transferred
facts.title: Hardware Security Guarantees

facts.statement: 1. **Attestation Integrity**: All system security checks are software-based and can be spoofed by a compromised system. Production deployments should use Managed Device Attestation (MDA) for hardware-attested security state.
facts.title: Software Security Considerations

facts.statement: 2. **JSON Determinism**: Attestation blobs use sorted JSON keys to ensure identical serialization between Swift and Go implementations for signature verification.
facts.title: Software Security Considerations

facts.statement: 3. **Memory Safety**: FFI bridge uses proper memory management with `Unmanaged` references and explicit cleanup functions.
facts.title: Software Security Considerations

facts.statement: - **Secure Enclave Bypass**: Not possible - private keys are hardware-isolated
facts.title: Attack Surface Analysis

facts.statement: - **Attestation Spoofing**: Possible for software-checked fields (SIP, Secure Boot) - mitigated by MDA in production
facts.title: Attack Surface Analysis

facts.statement: - **Signature Replay**: Mitigated by timestamp freshness checking in coordinator
facts.title: Attack Surface Analysis

facts.statement: - **FFI Memory Corruption**: Mitigated by careful memory management and Swift's type safety
facts.title: Attack Surface Analysis

facts.statement: - **coordinator/internal/attestation**: Uses the `attestation.VerifyJSON()` function to perform the core cryptographic verification of signed attestation blobs. This library handles P-256 ECDSA signature verification, JSON parsing with cross-language compatibility, and security policy enforcement (Secure Enclave required, SIP enabled, Secure Boot enabled). The verify-attestation service acts as a thin CLI wrapper around this verification logic.
